original_image_tags: Back of a worn, yellowed document page. The paper is creased from multiple folds and has torn edges. At the top center, a browned piece of tape with two punched holes covers some reversed text. Several vertical strips of translucent tape are visible, one on the left and two on the right. The main content consists of two stamped ink impressions.

elements.description: Scanned image of the back of a folded, aged paper document. Visible elements include faded blue ink stamps and a torn brown adhesive strip with two punch holes at the top. The stamps contain text such as 'RECEIVED', 'INTERNAL SECURITY', 'F.B.I.', 'RECEIVED-LEGAL', 'U.S. DEPT. OF JUSTICE', and dates like 'OCT 9 1947'. Faint reversed typed text bled through from the front of the page.

judge.reason: Description B claims specific, verbatim text from stamps (e.g., 'RECEIVED', 'INTERNAL SECURITY', 'F.B.I.', 'OCT 9 1947') that Description A completely omits, instead describing the stamps generically as 'two stamped ink impressions'.
